SPINOR / ALOFT / METRIC STORE / DEFINE A METRIC

Define a Metric — visual record

Proof the look was checked, not just the logic. The prototype rendered in a real browser across four combinations — light & dark, wide & narrow. This pass caught a preview-bar height bug that jsdom’s zero-error run had missed: the exact unit-green ≠ live gap the project exists to catch. Click any frame to enlarge.

What the machine still can’t sign off

These frames prove the render. They do not prove the behavior is wired to code — the surface is a design prototype. DefineMetricPanel, the applyOutcomeForUser semantic_authoring credit delta, the submit-audit CHECK constraint, the authoring-preview bypass, and synonym injection into context-builder remain [U] — unpinned against the real 3.5A–D source.

The verify-gate rule shown in the governance panel (verify advisory at capture, hard gate at promote) is a shared Teach+Store decision, but its gate set is four independent pins, not one — where confirmed is stored, how grounding is checked, how conflicts are represented, how steward is resolved. Enforcing three of four is a false-green. Pin order before code: the applyOutcomeForUser delta first — it is load-bearing for two surfaces, not one.

Rendered: headless Chromium, sRGB Themes: tokens shared with the prototype Companion to: Define a Metric (standalone).html
